summary: All two round-1 required fixes are correctly addressed. Three recommended findings were also resolved. No new security issues introduced. One residual observation (INFO, non-blocking) about gas_estimate_revert being a semantically approximate classification for a signature-parse failure, but this is inherent to the 7-reason taxonomy and is documented in the code. All 12 security invariants confirmed.

round1_required_findings_verification.description: v-normalization fallback no longer silently defaults to yParity=0.
round1_required_findings_verification.fix_analysis: settle.ts lines 356-366: the three-branch ladder is now explicit. Branch 1: `typeof sig.yParity === 'number'` uses it directly. Branch 2: `sig.v !== undefined` derives yParity as `Number(sig.v) - 27`. Branch 3: neither present — returns `{ ok: false, reason: 'gas_estimate_revert' }` without calling writeContract. This is correct: a signature that parseSignature cannot express as yParity or v is malformed and should not be broadcast. The choice of `gas_estimate_revert` as the reason is a best-fit within the constrained 7-reason taxonomy (there is no 'malformed_signature' bucket); the comment in the code documents this correctly.
round1_required_findings_verification.residual_note: gas_estimate_revert semantically means 'gas estimation reverted during pre-broadcast simulation'. A parseSignature edge case is not a gas estimation error. However, since the spec defines exactly 7 reason codes and this is the appropriate pre-broadcast failure bucket, the classification is acceptable. Documented in code.

round1_required_findings_verification.description: verify.ts now calls checkAndInsert exclusively; has+insert primitives are no longer called.
round1_required_findings_verification.fix_analysis: verify.ts lines 178-186: checkAndInsert is called with from, nonce, validBefore (in ms), and now. The result is checked: if !accepted, the reason ('nonce_already_used' | 'authorization_expired') is forwarded directly as the VerifyResult reason. Both strings are valid members of the VerifyReason union type (lines 63-70). The test in verify.test.ts lines 195-220 asserts checkSpy called once with expected args, hasSpy and insertSpy never called. The defense-in-depth property is now active: a validBefore <= now entry that somehow passes the earlier explicit check will be caught by checkAndInsert's internal guard. The checkAndInsert 'authorization_expired' path in verify.ts is unreachable under normal operation (the earlier explicit check at line ~148 already rejects validBeforeMs <= nowMs + SAFETY_MARGIN_MS, which is a stricter condition) — this is intentional defense-in-depth, correctly described in the comment.

round1_recommended_findings_verification.description: HttpRequestError (any status) now classified as rpc_5xx.
round1_recommended_findings_verification.fix_analysis: settle.ts lines 249-257: the status-conditional branches are removed; any HttpRequestError returns reason 'rpc_5xx'. The comment in the code correctly explains the rationale (429 rate-limit and 401 auth failures must not masquerade as rpc_timeout). This is accurate within the taxonomy constraints.

round1_recommended_findings_verification.description: Unknown network no longer throws NetworkMismatchError(0,0).
round1_recommended_findings_verification.fix_analysis: settle.ts lines 292-295: throws a plain Error with a descriptive message including the unknown network string. JSON.stringify is used on opts.network — since opts.network is a string, this is safe and produces a properly quoted value in the error message. No misleading D18 chain_id_mismatch event will be emitted for this case.

round1_recommended_findings_verification.description: WalletClient cache keyed only by network.id; second relayer key silently reused.
round1_recommended_findings_verification.fix_analysis: Per verify-settle's note, the single-key-per-process assumption is intentional per D6/D13. The code comment in buildWalletClient already documents 'Sole extraction site per D13 + systemic-fixes §5'. This is acceptable for MVP scope. Recommendation: add a short comment on the WALLET_CACHE declaration stating the per-process key assumption explicitly. No security issue in current scope.

new_findings.title: settle.ts balanceOf defensive type guard classifies non-bigint as rpc_5xx — correct but untested
new_findings.description: The defensive `typeof raw !== 'bigint'` check (new in round 2) correctly handles a misbehaving RPC or test double returning a non-bigint from readContract. The rpc_5xx classification is reasonable ('data plane is broken'). However, no test exercises this branch. For a new defensive branch touching the balance-check path, a missing test means coverage could slip.
new_findings.recommendation: Add a test: mock publicClient.readContract to return a string or number instead of bigint; assert result is { ok: false, reason: 'rpc_5xx' }. Low urgency — this is an RPC data-integrity guard, not a security control.

confirmed_invariants.description: EIP-712 domain built from NETWORKS[opts.expectedNetwork], not from payload
Tampered chainId / verifyingContract / name / version all produce invalid_signature
Time comparisons in milliseconds; SAFETY_MARGIN_MS=5_000; boundary nowSec+4 fails, nowSec+5 fails (exact boundary), nowSec+6 passes
confirmed_invariants.note: Round 2 adds the nowSec+5 exact-boundary test (verify.test.ts new lines). The check is `<=` (not strict `<`), confirmed: validBeforeMs == nowMs + 5_000 rejects. Three boundary tests now cover +4, +5, +6.
Fixed in round 2. verify.ts uses checkAndInsert. Test asserts checkSpy called once, hasSpy/insertSpy never called.

confirmed_invariants.description: settle.ts is sole getRelayerKeySecret() call site; raw key never crosses function boundaries
Proactive USDC.balanceOf check; constant MIN_RELAYER_USDC_BALANCE (1_000_000n); strict <
confirmed_invariants.note: Unchanged from round 1. Round 2 adds defensive non-bigint guard after the readContract call.

confirmed_invariants.description: authorization_already_used_onchain via case-insensitive substring match; no 4-byte selector
No SecurityLogger import or securityEvent call in verify.ts or settle.ts
confirmed_invariants.note: Unchanged from round 1. Static grep tests unchanged.

confirmed_invariants.description: settle.ts never touches NonceStore; replay-store retention on failure is structural
confirmed_invariants.note: Round 2 test refactored to exercise the full verify→settle pipeline and assert store.size() remains 1 after settle failure.

confirmed_invariants.description: ChainId pin on first writeContract per network; mismatch throws NetworkMismatchError
v/r/s split; v normalized to 27/28; malformed compact signature returns gas_estimate_revert
confirmed_invariants.note: Fixed in round 2. The fallback is now a hard failure return rather than silent yParity=0.

overall_assessment.secret_leak: CLEAN — no new paths introduced that could expose the relayer key. The OpaqueRelayerKey serialization test in round 2 also checks opts (not just result), expanding coverage.
overall_assessment.error_information_disclosure: CLEAN — no sensitive data in SettleResult failure shapes. Unknown network error now uses opts.network (a config string the developer wrote), not an internal address or key.
overall_assessment.signature_malleability: NOT_VULNERABLE — unchanged from round 1. v-normalization fix removes the edge case introduced in round 1.
overall_assessment.replay_protection: PROTECTED — checkAndInsert is now used, activating the defense-in-depth guard in NonceStore.
